policy.purpose: Macro and policy overlay for position sizing, cash level, and option risk.
policy.order_policy: Macro state can reduce risk, require review, or block new exposure. It must not create buy orders by itself.
policy.freshness_rule: Refresh Fed target range, rate-path bias, VIX, 10Y yield, and major political event risk before weekly rebalance.

current.notes: FOMC maintained target range; inflation elevated and Middle East developments contribute to high uncertainty.

indicator_watch.name: Fed funds target range
indicator_watch.interpretation: Higher-for-longer or renewed hikes reduce gross exposure and option risk.

indicator_watch.name: 10Y Treasury yield
indicator_watch.interpretation: Fast yield spikes are usually a headwind for long-duration growth stocks.

indicator_watch.name: VIX
indicator_watch.interpretation: Elevated volatility increases stop-out risk and lowers recommended gross exposure.

indicator_watch.name: US dollar index / liquidity proxy
indicator_watch.interpretation: A tightening liquidity backdrop lowers tolerance for concentrated satellite bets.

indicator_watch.name: Major policy/geopolitical events
indicator_watch.interpretation: Tariffs, export controls, elections, and sanctions can override normal technical signals.

UNKNOWN.note: When macro data is stale or unknown, avoid increasing leverage.

RISK_ON.note: Use only when trend and macro both support risk.

HAWKISH.note: Fed tightening/higher-for-longer regime: prefer quality, cash, and smaller satellite weights.

RISK_OFF.note: Macro or volatility shock: block new satellite exposure and review stops.
